title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the...
description: A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the component ushki Plugin. Performing a manipulation of the argument f_ushka_new/f_ushk results in cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. Upgrading to version 109.4 is recommended to address this issue. The patch is named 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The vendor was

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the 'ushki' plugin in MaxSite CMS up to version 109.3. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of websites using the affected CMS.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade MaxSite CMS to version 109.4 or apply the patch 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7.
Conduct a security audit of all websites using MaxSite CMS to identify potential exploitation of this vulnerability.
Implement web application firewalls (WAFs) to detect and block malicious XSS payloads until the patch is applied.

title: A weakness has been identified in SmythOS sre up to 0.0.15. This impacts an unknown function of...
description: A weakness has been identified in SmythOS sre up to 0.0.15. This impacts an unknown function of the file packages/sdk/src/LLM/utils.ts of the component Connector Service. This manipulation of the argument baseURL causes information disclosure. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in SmythOS sre up to version 0.0.15 allows for remote information disclosure via manipulation of the 'baseURL' argument in the Connector Service. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially exposing confidential information.
recommended_actions: Immediately update SmythOS sre to a version beyond 0.0.15, if available, or apply any available patches.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'baseURL' argument in the Connector Service to prevent unauthorized manipulation.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting the Connector Service, and review logs for signs of exploitation.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. The impacted element is the function...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. The impacted element is the function fromP2pListFilter of the file /goform/P2pListFilter. The manipulation of the argument menufacturer/Go leads to buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5 allows remote attackers to exploit a buffer overflow via the fromP2pListFilter function, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution, denial of service, or unauthorized access to the device.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches from Tenda to address CVE-2026-7019.
Restrict remote access to the device by configuring firewall rules and disabling unnecessary remote management features.
Monitor network traffic for signs of exploitation and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to block suspicious activity.

Performing a manipulation of the argument Name results in cross site scripting. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Datacom DM4100 Ethernet Configuration Page allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions within the affected system. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.

Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block XSS attempts.
Contact the vendor again to request a patch or mitigation guidance, and monitor for any updates or advisories.

title: OpenClaw: Agent gateway config mutations could change protected operator settings
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

The agent-facing `gateway config.patch` / `config.apply` guard did not cover several operator-trusted settings, including sandbox policy, plugin enablement, gateway auth/TLS, hook routing, MCP server configuration, SSRF policy, and filesystem hardening. A prompt-injected model with access to the owner-only gateway tool could persist changes to those settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Bundled MCP/LSP tools could bypass configured tool policy
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Bundled MCP and LSP tools could be appended to the agent's effective tool set after the normal tool-policy pipeline had already filtered core tools. If an operator configured a restrictive policy, such as a tool profile, explicit allow/deny list, owner-only tool restriction, sandbox tool policy, or subagent tool policy, a bundled MCP/LSP tool could remain ava

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the 'openclaw' npm package allows malicious actors to bypass restrictive tool policies by appending unauthorized MCP/LSP tools to the effective tool set. This could lead to unauthorized access, privilege escalation, or execution of malicious code, especially in environments relying on strict tool policies for security.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade the 'openclaw' npm package to version 2026.4.20 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and audit existing tool policies to ensure no unauthorized tools have been appended to the effective tool set.
Implement monitoring and logging for tool usage to detect any suspicious activity or policy bypass attempts.

title: OpenClaw: Workspace dotenv MiniMax host override could redirect credentialed requests
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `>= 2026.4.5, < 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

A malicious workspace `.env` could set `MINIMAX_API_HOST` and redirect credentialed MiniMax requests to an attacker-controlled origin, exposing the MiniMax API key in the outbound `Authorization` header.

This requires running OpenClaw from an attacker-controlled workspace. Severity is medium.

## Fix

OpenClaw now blocks `MINIMAX_API_HOST` from

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Browser CDP profile creation skipped strict-mode SSRF checks
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Browser profile creation normalized `cdpUrl` values before persisting them, but did not apply the configured browser SSRF policy at creation time. In deployments that explicitly disabled private-network CDP targets, a stored profile could still point at a private-network or metadata endpoint and later be probed by normal profile status flows.

Default trusted

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows bypassing strict-mode SSRF checks during browser profile creation, potentially enabling attackers to probe private-network or metadata endpoints. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive internal resources or metadata information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version `2026.4.20` or later to address the vulnerability.
Review and update SSRF policies to ensure they are enforced consistently across all stages of the application lifecycle.
Conduct a security audit of existing browser profiles to identify and remediate any potential misuse of `cdpUrl` values.

title: OpenClaw: Paired-device pairing actions were not limited to the caller device
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

A paired device session with limited pairing scope could enumerate global pairing state and act on pairing requests that belonged to another device within the same gateway scope ceiling.

This is a same-gateway paired-device authorization bug, not a remote unauthenticated issue. Severity is low.

## Fix

Pairing management actions are now limited to the calle

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a paired device within the same gateway to access and act on pairing requests intended for another device, potentially leading to unauthorized actions or data exposure. However, the issue is limited to same-gateway devices and does not involve remote or unauthenticated access.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version 2026.4.20 or later to address the vulnerability.
Review and audit device pairing configurations to ensure proper isolation and scope limitations.
Monitor gateway activity for any unusual or unauthorized pairing actions.

description: A vulnerability was determined in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: CVE-2026-0868 - The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's calendly shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 4.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0868.
